after: Non-dominant hand flat, palm facing body. Dominant hand flat, moves forward across the back of the non-dominant hand.
airplane: Extend thumb, index, and pinky fingers (ILY handshape). Move hand forward and slightly upward.
all: hold both hands out in front of you, palms up, and move them up
alligator: Extend both arms forward, palms facing each other. Clamp hands together repeatedly like an alligator's jaw.
animal: Fingertips of both slightly bent hands rest on chest. Pivot hands inward toward each other twice.
any: Right 'A' handshape with thumb extended pointing up, move hand downward and sweep to the right.
apple: Knuckle of the index finger of an 'X' handshape twists back and forth on the cheek.
arm: Sweep the palm of the dominant hand down the opposite arm.
aunt: An 'A' handshape with thumb extended is shaken in small circles near the lower cheek/jawline.
awake: Place 'L' handshapes with index fingers and thumbs pinched closed near eyes. Pop fingers open quickly to indicate eyes opening.
backyard: Sign 'BACK' (thumb points backward over shoulder) followed by 'YARD' (open flat hands sweep outward in a circle).
bad: Fingertips of a flat hand touch the chin, then hand pulls away and turns downward, palm facing the floor.
balloon: Start with closed fists touching near the mouth, expand hands outward and inflate cheeks as if a balloon is expanding.
bath: Both 'A' handshapes move up and down the chest in an alternating motion as if scrubbing oneself.
because: Index finger touches forehead, then pulls away while curling into an 'A' handshape.
bed: Place palms together and rest the side of the head on them, imitating sleeping on a pillow.
bedroom: Sign 'BED' (palms together at side of head) followed by 'ROOM' (flat hands form a box shape).
bee: Index finger and thumb pinch cheek (like a sting), then flat hand swats it away.
before: Non-dominant hand flat, palm facing body. Dominant hand flat, moves backward toward the body away from the non-dominant hand.
better: Flat hand touches chin, then pulls away and up into an 'A' handshape.
bird: Index finger and thumb pinch together repeatedly in front of the mouth, imitating a beak.
black: Draw the side of the index finger straight across the forehead.
blue: Make a 'B' handshape and twist the wrist left and right.
boat: the 3 handshape that represents a motorized land or water vehicle (car, bus, truck, van, or boat)
book: flat 'O' handshape and fingers spread, palm facing down
boy: Repeatedly pinch thumb and fingers together at the forehead, like grabbing the brim of a baseball cap.
brother: An 'L' handshape at forehead moves down to meet the non-dominant 'L' handshape pointing forward.
brown: Slide the side of a 'B' handshape down the cheek.
bug: Thumb rests on the nose while the index and middle fingers wiggle downward.
bye: Open hand facing forward, fold fingers down and up repeatedly in a waving motion.
callonphone: Make a 'Y' handshape (thumb and pinky extended), place thumb by the ear and pinky by the mouth.
can: Both 'S' handshapes (fists) move straight downward simultaneously.
car: make a flat 'C' shape with one hand, thumb on top of other fingers
carrot: Make an 'S' handshape near the mouth and move it slightly down and forward as if snapping a bite off a carrot.
cat: Pinch thumb and index fingers at the cheeks and pull outward, imitating whiskers.
cereal: Right index finger acts like a caterpillar crawling across the chin from left to right.
chair: Non-dominant 'U' handshape points horizontally. Dominant 'U' handshape taps downward on top of it twice.
cheek: The index finger is extended towards the cheek to indicate the location of an action.
chin: Point to or tap your own chin with your index finger.
chocolate: Dominant 'C' handshape circles over the back of the flat non-dominant hand.
clean: rub one hand over the other, palm to palm, in a smooth motion
close: Both 'B' handshapes face outward, then swing together so the sides of the index fingers touch.
closet: Non-dominant index finger points up. Dominant hand mimics hanging clothes on it with an 'X' handshape.
cloud: Both 'C' handshapes circle each other while moving across in front of the body.
clown: Thumb and fingers form a claw shape and squeeze the nose twice.
cow: Place the thumb of a 'Y' handshape against the temple and twist the wrist forward to imitate horns.
cry: Place index fingers below the eyes and trace downward on the cheeks, mimicking tears.
cut: Simulate cutting motion with dominant hand, e.g. slicing through air.
cute: Index and middle fingers of a 'U' handshape brush downward against the chin and fold into the palm.
dad: Tap the thumb of an open '5' handshape against the center of the forehead.
dance: Dominant 'V' handshape points downward and swings side to side over the flat, upward-facing non-dominant palm.
dirty: Place the back of the hand under the chin and wiggle the fingers.
dog: Pat the leg twice, then snap the fingers (or just pat the leg or snap depending on regional variation).
doll: Stroke an 'X' handshape downward on the nose twice.
donkey: Place open flat hands at the sides of the head with thumbs touching temples, bend all fingers forward twice.
drawer: Both hands in 'S' handshapes (fists) mimic grabbing handles and pulling a drawer open.
drink: Hold a 'C' handshape near the mouth and tilt it upward as if drinking from a cup.
drop: Hold both hands in 'S' handshapes, move them downward while opening them quickly into '5' handshapes.
dry: Pull the index finger of an 'X' handshape horizontally across the chin from left to right.
dryer: Sign 'DRY' (pulling 'X' across chin) followed by 'MACHINE' (fingers interlocked and moving up and down).
duck: Open and close the index/middle fingers against the thumb in front of the mouth, like a duck's bill.
ear: Point to or pull gently on your own earlobe.
elephant: Hold flat hand in front of the nose and outline the shape of an elephant's trunk curving downward and up.
empty: Hold open palm of one hand and then close it.
eye: Point to your own eye with your index finger.
face: Trace a circle around your own face with your index finger.
fall: Dominant 'V' handshape stands on flat non-dominant palm, then flips over to land on its back.
farm: Sweep the thumb of an open '5' handshape across the chin from one side to the other.
fast: the dominant hand moves quickly forward and backward in front of the body
feet: Point downwards toward your feet with both index fingers.
find: Start with an open '5' handshape, pick up an imaginary object by bringing the thumb and index finger together while pulling the hand upward.
fine: Smile and nod head, then hold hands together in a fist (representing being fine or okay)
finger: Hold up one hand and point to its fingers with the index finger of the other hand.
finish: Move dominant hand downwards, palm facing forward, as if finishing something.
fireman: Place the back of a flat 'B' handshape horizontally against the forehead, representing a helmet badge.
first: extend your index finger on one hand and move it forward, keeping other fingers closed
fish: Hold dominant flat hand pointing forward, wave it back and forth as it moves forward to mimic a swimming fish.
flag: Non-dominant hand acts as a base; dominant elbow rests on it while the flat dominant hand waves back and forth like a flag.
flower: Touch the fingertips of a squished 'O' handshape to one side of the nose, then the other.
food: Tap the fingertips of a squished 'O' handshape against the lips twice.
for: Touch index finger to the forehead, then swing it outward and forward.
frenchfries: Bounce an 'F' handshape twice while moving it slightly to the side.
frog: Flick the index and middle fingers out from beneath the chin twice.
garbage: Tap the wrist of a dominant 'D' or pointing hand against the forehead, then flick it toward the floor (regional variations exist, commonly pointing to elbow).
gift: Both hands in 'X' handshapes move outward from the body as if handing something to someone.
giraffe: Make a 'C' handshape with the dominant hand and move it up the neck to above the head, showing a long neck.
girl: Stroke the thumb of an 'A' handshape down the jawline from the ear to the chin.
give: Mimic giving something to someone else, often done with an open palm and arm extended
glasswindow: Both hands open, palms facing body; dominant hand rests above non-dominant hand. Dominant hand moves up, then back down to touch the non-dominant hand.
go: Point both index fingers outward and move them forward.
goose: Make a larger beak shape with the whole hand (fingers to thumb) opening and closing in front of the mouth.
grandma: Thumb of an open '5' handshape touches the chin, then the hand bounces forward in two small arcs.
grandpa: Thumb of an open '5' handshape touches the forehead, then the hand bounces forward in two small arcs.
grass: Push the heel of an open, upward-facing hand gently upward against the chin.
green: Make a 'G' handshape and twist the wrist back and forth.
gum: Bend the index and middle fingers and twist them against the cheek.
hair: Pinch a lock of your own hair with your thumb and index finger.
happy: Brush flat hands upward on the chest in small circular motions.
hat: Pat the top of the head twice with a flat hand.
hate: Flick the middle fingers of both hands outward from the thumbs, pushing hands away from the body.
have: make a grasping motion with your hand as if holding something
head: Tap a bent flat hand on the side of the head, then lower it and tap the jaw/cheek.
hear: Point to or tap the ear with the index finger.
helicopter: Dominant open '5' handshape facing down flutters on top of the upright thumb of the non-dominant '3' handshape.
hello: Wave your hand towards the person you are greeting
hen: Sign 'BIRD' (thumb and index finger pinch at mouth) then tap the flat hand downwards into the non-dominant palm.
hesheit: Point the index finger out to the side or forward at the person or object being referred to.
hide: Bring an 'A' handshape (thumb tucked) down to the lips, then move it under the curved non-dominant hand.
high: Move a flat hand, palm facing downward, up to a high level.
home: Bring a squished 'O' handshape to the mouth/chin area, then touch it to the upper cheek/ear area.
horse: Thumb of 'H' handshape touches the temple, while the index and middle fingers bend forward twice.
hot: Form a claw handshape over the mouth, then turn it outward and throw it down quickly.
hungry: A sign made by holding the stomach area with both hands, indicating hunger
icecream: Hold an 'S' handshape near the mouth and make a licking motion downwards.
if: Tap the pinky finger of an 'I' handshape against the upper cheek/corner of the eye twice.
into: Slide the fingertips of the dominant flat hand inside the semi-closed non-dominant 'C' handshape.
jacket: Both 'A' handshapes trace down the chest from the shoulders, as if putting on a coat.
jump: Dominant 'V' handshape stands on flat non-dominant palm, then bends its 'legs' and springs upward.
kiss: Gently touch tips of index fingers and thumbs of both hands together in a light pressing motion.
kitty: Pinch thumb and index fingers at the cheeks and pull outward (same as 'CAT').
lamp: Dominant open '5' handshape facing down opens and closes (flashes) above the non-dominant hand or head.
later: Hold non-dominant hand flat; dominant 'L' handshape rests thumb on it, then index finger pivots down to point forward.
like: Extend dominant index finger upwards, then bend it down slightly while keeping it extended. Hold this position for a brief moment before returning to the starting position.
lion: A curved open handshape moves backward over the top of the head, imitating a mane.
lips: Trace the outline of your lips with your index finger.
listen: Cup a hand behind your ear.
look: Extend dominant index finger, then point it in the direction of something you want to draw attention to. Keep your gaze directed at that thing.
loud: Hold one hand out in front of you, palm facing downwards. Move your other hand in a sharp, zigzag motion above the first hand, as if conducting sound waves.
mad: Rake fingers through hair with both hands, then throw your head back and make a loud noise.
make: Stack both fists (S handshapes) on top of each other and twist them.
many: Hold fists facing upward, then flick all fingers open quickly multiple times.
milk: Open and close the dominant fist rhythmically, as if milking a cow.
mom: Tap the thumb of an open '5' handshape against the chin.
moon: Hold a 'C' handshape near the eye/forehead and move it upward and outward.
morning: Non-dominant arm lies horizontally; dominant flat hand moves upward from underneath it, like a sunrise.
mouse: Brush the tip of the index finger back and forth across the tip of the nose.
mouth: Draw a circle around your mouth with your index finger.
nap: Sign 'SLEEP' (open hand draws down over face into pinched fingers) followed by a short duration marker.
napkin: Wipe a flat hand across the mouth from side to side.
night: Make a circular motion above head with dominant hand.
no: move hands apart and up slightly to indicate 'no'
noisy: the dominant hand makes a loud motion, as if speaking loudly
nose: Point to your nose with your index finger.
not: Thumb of an 'A' handshape starts under the chin and pulls straight forward.
now: Bring both 'Y' handshapes down slightly in front of the body.
nuts: Flick the thumb of an 'A' handshape outward from behind the upper front teeth.
old: the dominant hand touches the chin, then moves downward to indicate age
on: Place the dominant flat hand down onto the back of the non-dominant flat hand.
open: Both flat hands start side-by-side facing forward, then swing apart to face outwards like opening double doors.
orange: Squeeze the hand into a fist (like squeezing an orange) in front of the chin repeatedly.
outside: Open hand near the face moves outward and pinches closed into an 'O' handshape.
owl: Both hands form 'O' handshapes over the eyes, then twist inward and outward.
pajamas: Sign 'SLEEP' (hand draws down over face) followed by 'CLOTHES' (thumbs brush down chest).
pen: Mimic holding a pen with thumb and index finger and make a scribbling motion on the flat non-dominant palm.
pencil: Touch index finger to mouth (licking lead), then make a scribbling motion on the flat non-dominant palm.
penny: Index finger touches the forehead and pulls away slightly.
person: Both flat hands face each other and move straight down, tracing the sides of a body.
pig: Place the back of a flat hand under the chin and bend the fingers down and up repeatedly.
pizza: Dominant hand uses a bent 'V' handshape to trace a 'Z' shape in the air (or mimic holding a slice to the mouth).
please: move hands apart and up slightly
police: Tap a 'C' handshape over the heart, representing a police badge.
pool: Sign 'WATER' (W handshape to chin) then trace a large rectangle in the air with index fingers.
potty: Make a 'T' handshape (thumb between index and middle finger) and shake it back and forth.
pretend: Use your index finger and thumb of your dominant hand to mimic picking up an object, while moving it back and forth
pretty: Open hand circles the face and closes into an 'And' handshape at the chin.
puppy: Sign 'DOG' (pat leg/snap) but with smaller, gentler movements, often adding 'BABY'.
puzzle: Both hands interlock fingers and move around, mimicking fitting puzzle pieces together.
quiet: Place index finger to lips ('shh'), then move both flat hands outward and downwards facing the floor.
radio: Hold an 'A' handshape or a 'C' handshape up to the ear, as if holding an old transistor radio, and turn a dial.
rain: Both hands with fingers pointing down move downward in a jerky motion, imitating falling rain.
read: Dominant 'V' handshape (eyes) scans up and down the flat non-dominant hand (the page).
red: Draw a vertical line with one hand, palm down, and make an arrow shape pointing downward
refrigerator: Sign 'COLD' (shiver with fists) followed by 'DOOR' (swinging flat hands open).
room: Both flat hands face each other, then form the parallel sides of a box, representing walls.
sad: Hold both hands with open fingers in front of the face, then slowly pull them downward, adopting a sad facial expression.
same: Extend thumb and pinky ('Y' handshape) and slide the hand horizontally back and forth between two objects/people.
say: Tap the index finger repeatedly against the chin, pointing horizontally towards the mouth.
scissors: Make a 'V' handshape and open/close the index and middle fingers like cutting with scissors.
see: Make a 'window' shape with your dominant hand by separating your thumb and index finger, and then move it as if looking through a window.
shhh: Place the index finger vertically against the center of the lips.
shirt: Pinch the fabric of your shirt at the upper chest with your thumb and index finger and pull it outward slightly.
shoe: Both 'S' handshapes (fists) tap together at the thumbs twice.
shower: Hold an 'O' handshape above the head and open the fingers out while moving hand down, imitating water spraying.
sick: Make a circular motion above head with dominant hand.
sleep: Start with an open hand over the face, draw it down to the chin while pulling fingers together into an 'O', closing eyes.
smile: Use index fingers to trace the upward curve of a smile at the corners of the mouth.
snack: Tap the fingers of a pinched 'O' handshape lightly against the mouth/chin area multiple times.
snow: Move both hands up and down, as if throwing snowballs.
stairs: Index and middle fingers of dominant hand 'walk' up the air like climbing steps.
stay: Hold both hands in 'Y' handshapes (or flat hands) pointing forward, and push them down slightly.
sticky: Touch thumbs and middle fingers together, pull them apart and together again, showing stickiness.
store: Hold both pinched 'O' handshapes pointing down in front of the body, swing them forward and back a few times.
story: Mimic telling a story by moving both hands in a circular motion, as if holding a pen or pencil.
stuck: Place a 'V' handshape firmly into the neck/throat area.
sun: Mimic holding an invisible sun with your dominant hand while smiling.
table: Rest the dominant flat forearm horizontally across the non-dominant flat forearm.
talk: Place the side of a '4' handshape near the mouth and move it backward and forward.
taste: Tap the middle finger of an open hand lightly on the tongue or lower lip.
thankyou: Touch the fingertips of a flat hand to the chin, then move the hand forward and down toward the person.
that: Place a 'Y' handshape firmly down onto the flat non-dominant palm.
think: Rub your chin or forehead with the dominant hand while making a thinking expression.
thirsty: Trace the index finger down the throat from the chin to the chest.
tiger: Both clawed hands start at the center of the face and pull outwards, representing stripes.
time: Mimic looking at an imaginary clock or watch on your wrist and moving it forward.
tomorrow: Hold up one hand and mimic looking at an imaginary clock or watch on your wrist, pointing to the future.
tongue: Point to your tongue with your index finger.
tooth: Mimic brushing your teeth with an imaginary toothbrush while making a healthy expression.
toothbrush: Move an index finger horizontally back and forth in front of the teeth.
touch: Mimic touching something or someone with an imaginary finger.
toy: Hold both hands in 'T' handshapes and twist the wrists inward and outward repeatedly.
tree: Non-dominant arm lies horizontally; dominant elbow rests on it with a '5' handshape waving, representing branches.
uncle: Tap the side of the non-dominant fist with the tip of the index finger
underwear: Index fingers and thumbs trace the elastic line of underwear near the hips/groin area.
up: Point the index finger straight upward.
vacuum: Dominant fist moves back and forth over the flat non-dominant palm, as if vacuuming a floor.
wait: Hold up a flat palm facing outwards.
wake: Sign 'AWAKE' (pinched 'L' handshapes pop open near the eyes).
water: Tap the index finger of a 'W' handshape against the chin a few times.
wet: Sign 'WATER' (W to chin) then pull both hands downwards, closing fingers to thumbs as if wringing out water.
weus: Index finger touches the dominant shoulder, then arcs across the chest to touch the non-dominant shoulder.
where: Hold up both hands, palms facing each other, and move them apart as if opening doors.
white: Place an open hand on the chest and pull it away while pinching all fingers together.
who: Place thumb on the chin, index finger bends and unbends repeatedly.
why: Shake head from side to side
will: Place flat hand with palm facing side against the cheek/ear, then move it straight forward.
wolf: Open hand starts at the face and pulls out to form a snout, closing into pinched fingers.
yellow: Make a 'Y' handshape and twist the wrist back and forth.
yesterday: Thumb of an 'A' handshape touches the chin, then arcs back to touch the cheek near the ear.
zebra: Sign 'HORSE', then use both hands to trace horizontal stripes across the chest.
zipper: Mimic holding a zipper pull with thumb and index finger and move it up the center of the chest.
